__meta__.description: Phase 5 Industry Context Library — injects domain-specific vocabulary, regulations, and prompt preambles into all Gemini validation calls

vocabulary.PSP: Payment Service Provider — intermediary processing card transactions
vocabulary.acquirer: Bank that processes payments on behalf of merchant
vocabulary.issuer: Bank that issues payment cards to consumers
vocabulary.chargeback: Forced reversal of a card transaction initiated by the issuing bank
vocabulary.tokenization: Replacing sensitive card data with a non-sensitive token (PCI requirement)
vocabulary.PAN: Primary Account Number — the 16-digit card number
vocabulary.CVV: Card Verification Value — 3/4-digit security code on card
vocabulary.3DS: 3-D Secure — additional authentication layer for online card payments
vocabulary.gateway: Service routing payment authorization requests between merchant and acquirer
vocabulary.settlement: Transfer of funds from acquirer to merchant bank
vocabulary.refund: Merchant-initiated reversal of a completed payment
vocabulary.idempotency: Property ensuring duplicate payment requests produce same result without double charge
vocabulary.webhook: Real-time HTTP callback notifying merchant of payment status changes

fintech/payments.prompt_injection:
=== TENANT CONTEXT: FINTECH / PAYMENTS ===
This system processes financial payments. Apply PCI-DSS standards rigorously.
Key compliance areas: card data tokenization, no PAN storage post-auth, TLS 1.2+ on all payment endpoints, webhook signature verification, idempotency on charge endpoints.
Flag ANY requirement that involves storing raw card data (PAN, CVV) as a CRITICAL security violation.
=== END TENANT CONTEXT ===

vocabulary.APR: Annual Percentage Rate — total cost of borrowing expressed as yearly rate
vocabulary.origination: Process of creating a new loan from application to disbursal
vocabulary.underwriting: Risk assessment process to determine loan eligibility and terms
vocabulary.credit bureau: Agency compiling credit history (CIBIL, Equifax, Experian)
vocabulary.CIBIL score: Credit score ranging 300-900 used by Indian lenders
vocabulary.NPA: Non-Performing Asset — loan with principal/interest overdue > 90 days
vocabulary.EMI: Equated Monthly Installment — fixed monthly repayment amount
vocabulary.disbursement: Transfer of approved loan funds to borrower
vocabulary.collateral: Asset pledged as security for a loan
vocabulary.LTV: Loan-to-Value ratio — loan amount as percentage of asset value
vocabulary.delinquency: Failure to make scheduled payment on time
vocabulary.soft pull: Credit inquiry that does not affect credit score
vocabulary.hard pull: Credit inquiry that temporarily reduces credit score

fintech/lending.prompt_injection:
=== TENANT CONTEXT: FINTECH / LENDING ===
This system manages loan origination and servicing. Key compliance areas: TILA disclosure of APR and fees before loan agreement, FCRA requirements on credit bureau data handling, BSA/AML checks on borrower identity, no discriminatory lending (ECOA).
Flag any endpoint that calculates interest/fees without explicit APR disclosure.
=== END TENANT CONTEXT ===

vocabulary.KYC: Know Your Customer — identity verification before account opening
vocabulary.AML: Anti-Money Laundering — controls to detect and prevent money laundering
vocabulary.RTGS: Real-Time Gross Settlement — instant large-value interbank transfer system
vocabulary.NEFT: National Electronic Funds Transfer — batch-based fund transfer in India
vocabulary.UPI: Unified Payments Interface — Indian real-time payment system via VPA
vocabulary.SWIFT: Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication
vocabulary.nostro: Bank's account held at a foreign bank in foreign currency
vocabulary.core banking: Centralized system processing all bank transactions in real-time
vocabulary.CASA: Current Account Savings Account — low-cost deposit base for banks
vocabulary.provisioning: Setting aside reserves for potential loan losses (NPA)
vocabulary.reconciliation: Process of matching internal records with counterparty records

banking.prompt_injection:
=== TENANT CONTEXT: BANKING ===
This system operates in a regulated banking environment. Mandatory areas: KYC/AML checks before account activation, transaction monitoring for suspicious activity (BSA), RBI-compliant data residency, audit trails for all financial operations, multi-factor authentication for privileged actions.
Flag any transaction endpoint without idempotency or audit logging.
=== END TENANT CONTEXT ===

vocabulary.PHI: Protected Health Information — individually identifiable health data (HIPAA)
vocabulary.EHR: Electronic Health Record — digital version of patient's medical history
vocabulary.EMR: Electronic Medical Record — practice-specific patient record
vocabulary.FHIR: Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources — modern HL7 API standard
vocabulary.HL7: Health Level 7 — healthcare data exchange standards organization
vocabulary.ICD-10: International Classification of Diseases — medical diagnosis coding system
vocabulary.CPT: Current Procedural Terminology — medical procedure billing codes
vocabulary.DICOM: Digital Imaging and Communications in Medicine — medical imaging standard
vocabulary.audit trail: Immutable chronological record of all PHI access (HIPAA requirement)
vocabulary.de-identification: Removing all 18 PHI identifiers so data is no longer protected
vocabulary.consent: Patient authorization for data sharing or specific treatments
vocabulary.BAA: Business Associate Agreement — HIPAA contract with third-party vendors

healthcare.prompt_injection:
=== TENANT CONTEXT: HEALTHCARE ===
This system handles Protected Health Information (PHI). HIPAA compliance is mandatory.
Critical requirements: encryption at rest and in transit for ALL PHI, audit logs for every PHI access/modification, minimum necessary access principle, BAA required before any PHI sharing with third parties, no PHI in logs or error messages.
Flag any endpoint that returns patient data without access control checks.
=== END TENANT CONTEXT ===

vocabulary.multi-tenancy: Architecture serving multiple customers from shared infrastructure with data isolation
vocabulary.tenant isolation: Guarantee that one tenant cannot access another's data
vocabulary.SLA: Service Level Agreement — contractual uptime/performance commitment
vocabulary.RBAC: Role-Based Access Control — permissions tied to user roles
vocabulary.SSO: Single Sign-On — one login credential for multiple services (SAML/OAuth)
vocabulary.webhook: HTTP callback delivering real-time event notifications to subscribers
vocabulary.rate limiting: Throttling API requests to prevent abuse and ensure fair usage
vocabulary.idempotency: Repeating the same API call produces the same result without side effects
vocabulary.soft delete: Marking records as deleted without removing from database
vocabulary.audit log: Immutable record of all user actions for compliance and debugging
vocabulary.feature flag: Toggle to enable/disable features per tenant or user segment

saas.prompt_injection:
=== TENANT CONTEXT: SAAS ===
This is a multi-tenant SaaS application. Key validation areas: every data query must filter by tenant_id, no cross-tenant data leakage, RBAC enforcement on all endpoints, audit logging for destructive operations, idempotency on mutation endpoints, rate limiting on public APIs.
Flag any DB query missing tenant_id isolation as CRITICAL.
=== END TENANT CONTEXT ===

vocabulary.SKU: Stock Keeping Unit — unique identifier for each distinct product variant
vocabulary.cart abandonment: User adds items to cart but leaves without completing purchase
vocabulary.checkout funnel: Sequence of steps from cart to completed order
vocabulary.fulfillment: Process of picking, packing, and shipping an order
vocabulary.inventory: Stock of products available for sale
vocabulary.backorder: Order for out-of-stock item to be fulfilled when stock arrives
vocabulary.return: Customer-initiated reversal of a completed purchase
vocabulary.refund: Repayment to customer after return or complaint
vocabulary.AOV: Average Order Value — total revenue divided by number of orders
vocabulary.conversion rate: Percentage of visitors who complete a purchase
vocabulary.wishlist: Saved list of products user intends to buy later
vocabulary.promo code: Alphanumeric code granting discount at checkout

ecommerce.prompt_injection:
=== TENANT CONTEXT: E-COMMERCE ===
This is an e-commerce platform. Key validation areas: PCI-DSS for payment processing, GDPR/CCPA consent before marketing emails (CAN-SPAM), inventory consistency (no overselling), order idempotency, customer PII protection.
Flag any product/pricing endpoint missing input validation as a potential injection vector.
=== END TENANT CONTEXT ===

vocabulary.AWB: Air Waybill — shipping document for air cargo
vocabulary.BOL: Bill of Lading — receipt and contract between shipper and carrier
vocabulary.POD: Proof of Delivery — confirmation shipment was received by consignee
vocabulary.last-mile: Final leg of delivery from local hub to customer address
vocabulary.WMS: Warehouse Management System — controls warehouse operations
vocabulary.TMS: Transport Management System — optimizes freight movement
vocabulary.manifest: List of cargo contents for a shipment or vehicle
vocabulary.dwell time: Duration cargo waits at a facility before next movement
vocabulary.SLA breach: Delivery outside the contractual time window

logistics.prompt_injection:
=== TENANT CONTEXT: LOGISTICS ===
This system manages shipment and delivery operations. Key validation areas: chain of custody tracking (immutable scan events), GPS coordinate validation, SLA breach alerting, hazardous material compliance flags, customs documentation completeness.
Flag any tracking endpoint that allows backdating scan events.
=== END TENANT CONTEXT ===

vocabulary.linting: Static analysis to catch style and logic errors before runtime
vocabulary.SAST: Static Application Security Testing — analyzing code for vulnerabilities
vocabulary.DAST: Dynamic Application Security Testing — testing running application
vocabulary.monorepo: Single repository containing multiple projects or packages
vocabulary.trunk-based development: All developers integrate to main branch frequently
vocabulary.feature flag: Code toggle to enable features for subset of users
vocabulary.observability: Ability to understand system state from logs, metrics, traces
vocabulary.SLO: Service Level Objective — internal performance target
vocabulary.dependency graph: Map of relationships between software packages
vocabulary.SBOM: Software Bill of Materials — inventory of all software components

devtools.prompt_injection:
=== TENANT CONTEXT: DEVELOPER TOOLS ===
This is a developer tooling platform. Key validation areas: API key scoping (least privilege), webhook signature verification, rate limiting on compute-heavy endpoints, OSS license compatibility checks, audit logs for sensitive operations (delete, admin actions).
Flag any public API endpoint lacking authentication or rate limiting.
=== END TENANT CONTEXT ===
